jokes.text: I just farted.
jokes.setup: Jeremy greets Leslie enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Immediately announces he just farted
jokes.explanation: Low-brow humor that fits Jamm's crude character but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: and human equivalent of gas station sushi
jokes.setup: Describing Jeremy Jamm
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to gas station sushi - questionable and potentially harmful
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that's specific and evocative - perfectly captures Jamm's nature

jokes.text: Jammy.
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes they're a couple
jokes.punchline: Their portmanteau name sounds like a food product
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that works but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Why? Dear God, why?
jokes.setup: Learning about Jamm and Tammy's relationship
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified existential questioning
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction that feels genuine to Leslie's horror

jokes.text: Hit it off at karaoke. She saw me nail Gangsta's Paradise, I saw her bite the door guy.
jokes.setup: Explaining how they met
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal karaoke to Tammy biting security
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that perfectly captures both characters' personalities

jokes.text: And you can trust my opinion because I have a lot to gain by being right, and I have severe tunnel vision about achieving my goals.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to convince someone
jokes.punchline: Admits her obvious bias as a reason to trust her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Leslie acknowledges her flaws as strengths

jokes.text: because it's a hotel in Los Angeles, not a wine.
jokes.setup: Joan requested Chateau Marmont
jokes.punchline: It's a hotel, not wine
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke that highlights Joan's eccentricity

jokes.text: No, because he's been famously dead for 60 years.
jokes.setup: Joan asks if they got Buddy Holly to sing
jokes.punchline: Deadpan explanation of why that's impossible
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery, 'famously dead' is a nice touch

jokes.text: You said your name was Glenn? Yes, I did. That's pretty.
jokes.setup: April gives fake name to Joan
jokes.punchline: Joan mishears and compliments the wrong name
jokes.explanation: Shows Joan's declining mental state through simple miscommunication

jokes.text: She once gave every member in her audience a car. One car, that they all had to fight for.
jokes.setup: Sounds like generous Oprah-style giveaway
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was one car for everyone to fight over
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of Oprah trope - sets up expectation then destroys it brilliantly

jokes.text: Tom. What does culinary mean? It means it's a cause for celebration.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what 'culinary' means
jokes.punchline: Tom confidently gives wrong definition based on sound similarity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's fake confidence covering ignorance

jokes.text: Let me also get a chicken parm and a lasagna on the side. And a spaghetti to go. On the house!
jokes.setup: Tom offers drinks on the house
jokes.punchline: Customer immediately escalates to ordering expensive food
jokes.explanation: Relatable escalation showing how free offers get abused

jokes.text: In my experience with butt faces, you are one.
jokes.setup: Ron makes condescending comment about capitalism
jokes.punchline: Leslie's childish but delivered-seriously comeback
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - childish insult delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Please don't say Hitler. Adolf Hitler! I'm with Swanson. It's a stache thing. Stache bros.
jokes.setup: Leslie pleads Jamm not to say Hitler
jokes.punchline: He immediately says Hitler then bonds with Ron over mustaches
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with absurd mustache brotherhood logic

jokes.text: Thank you, Commissioner Gordon, people of Gotham. Okay. She thinks she's in Batman.
jokes.setup: Joan gives confused speech
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact diagnosis of Joan's delusion
jokes.explanation: April's deadpan observation of Joan's mental decline is perfectly delivered

jokes.text: On a separate note, I think that America should have a purge night. Let me explain why.
jokes.setup: Joan giving inspirational speech about following passion
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to advocating for a murder night
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy pivot from inspiration to sociopathy

jokes.text: I must be the first person ever to have money, power, and notoriety, but still feel empty.
jokes.setup: Tom feeling unfulfilled despite success
jokes.punchline: Claims to be first rich person ever to feel empty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's self-centeredness and lack of perspective

jokes.text: That's my cousin. How about her? That's my dad!
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting women for Tom to date
jokes.punchline: Escalates from cousin to suggesting his dad
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke showing Andy's confusion about dating apps

jokes.text: Why are you only friends with your family?
jokes.setup: Seeing Tom's social media connections
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent question about Tom's social isolation
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent observation highlights Tom's loneliness in a funny way

jokes.text: Think about what you're saying right now. 'Cause it's the smartest idea anyone's ever had!
jokes.setup: Seems like Andy will be voice of reason
jokes.punchline: Completely reverses and endorses the crazy plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - appears wise then reveals his impulsiveness

jokes.text: And I know this because earlier I sneaked and snooked around and Jammy was supposed to vote for me! The snooker has become the snorked!
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her investigation
jokes.punchline: Makes up increasingly nonsensical sneak-related words
jokes.explanation: Excellent Leslie wordplay showing her tendency to get carried away with language

jokes.text: Are you horny with gratitude?
jokes.setup: Tammy asking if her vote manipulation worked
jokes.punchline: Phrases it in the most uncomfortable way possible
jokes.explanation: Peak Tammy - combining manipulation with sexual crudeness in perfect character

jokes.text: It's like rescuing a bunny from a lion. But that lion is a demonic sociopath with really nice cleavage.
jokes.setup: Leslie comparing the rescue mission to saving a bunny
jokes.punchline: Adds specific details about Tammy's evil and attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good extended metaphor that captures both the situation and Leslie's verbose style

jokes.text: Chicago! The big apple.
jokes.setup: Andy excited about arriving in Chicago
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'The Big Apple' which is New York's nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy geographical confusion delivered with typical enthusiasm

jokes.text: That's what murderers do before they murder someone.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why you can't show up unannounced
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to murderer behavior
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor observation about stalker behavior

jokes.text: You're not gonna murder me, are you?
jokes.setup: Tom's earlier comment about murderers
jokes.punchline: Lucy immediately asks about murder when Tom appears
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the murderer comment was accurate about how this would look

jokes.text: Head coach of the Chicago Bulls. No, he's working at a non-profit. No. What? Am I?
jokes.setup: Andy lies about Tom's job
jokes.punchline: Andy gets confused by his own lie and Tom's correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - lies to help, then gets confused by his own story

jokes.text: I'm just gonna go live under a bridge and ask people riddles before they cross.
jokes.setup: April feeling lost about her career
jokes.punchline: Plans to become a literal troll from fairy tales
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April line - perfectly absurd yet somehow fitting for her dark worldview

jokes.text: That it doesn't count as stripping if no one pays you? Yes, she did say that.
jokes.setup: Ben asks what April remembers from Joan's speech
jokes.punchline: April recalls the most inappropriate part instead of the inspiration
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing April focuses on the darkest details

jokes.text: And I'm scared of death.
jokes.setup: Ben introducing April at the funeral home
jokes.punchline: April's blunt admission while visiting a place that handles death
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - inappropriate honesty in professional setting

jokes.text: People are dying to get in.
jokes.setup: Welcoming visitors to funeral home
jokes.punchline: Classic death pun
jokes.explanation: Extremely predictable funeral home pun that's barely above groan level

jokes.text: She has my stool analyzed just to keep me honest.
jokes.setup: Explaining Tammy's control over his diet
jokes.punchline: Reveals extremely invasive monitoring
jokes.explanation: Gross but effective way to show how controlling Tammy is

jokes.text: But, you know, it's good, you know? I'm better now. I mean, sure, I'm depressed, and I'm constantly sick, and nothing really brings me joy. But it just feels right.
jokes.setup: Jamm claiming the relationship is good
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly horrible symptoms while insisting it's fine
jokes.explanation: Perfect abusive relationship psychology - rationalizing obvious misery as 'right'

jokes.text: Hey, look at that. More hair came out.
jokes.setup: Jamm is stressed and sick from relationship
jokes.punchline: Hair falling out in real time
jokes.explanation: Visual confirmation of how toxic the relationship is

jokes.text: You can watch Hitch 2 anytime, anywhere! Finally! That's the dream.
jokes.setup: Tom describing Gryzzl's benefits
jokes.punchline: Lucy's enthusiasm for a presumably bad sequel
jokes.explanation: Simple joke about bad movie sequels and low entertainment standards

jokes.text: Dammit, Ron! Engage in the culture once in a while!
jokes.setup: Ron hasn't seen Monsters, Inc.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's exasperated demand he participate in pop culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie frustration with Ron's cultural isolation

jokes.text: You keep that in your desk?
jokes.setup: Ron pulls out a gold bar
jokes.punchline: Leslie's casual surprise at this extreme office item
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Ron's extreme preparedness/wealth

jokes.text: We haven't done the deed yet. She lets me rub up against her leg sometimes.
jokes.setup: Discussing lack of sexual activity
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetically limited physical contact
jokes.explanation: Cringey but effective way to show how little Tammy gives him

jokes.text: Why is every job just paperwork?
jokes.setup: Learning mortician work is mostly filing
jokes.punchline: April's frustrated universal observation
jokes.explanation: Relatable millennial frustration about boring work realities

jokes.text: being embalmed by some weirdo who had to go to school for three years just to cut my guts open.
jokes.setup: April's existential career crisis
jokes.punchline: Visualizes her own death while insulting the mortician
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor - morbid thoughts delivered casually

jokes.text: Hey there, horsey. Time to mount up and ride on into Bonertown.
jokes.setup: Leslie role-playing as Tammy
jokes.punchline: Hilariously inappropriate sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying to roleplay Tammy's sexuality is perfectly uncomfortable and funny

jokes.text: To hell with you woman, goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ron practicing Jamm's responses
jokes.punchline: Ron's version is way too confident and articulate for Jamm
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Ron's dignity and Jamm's actual pathetic nature

jokes.text: It's a crotch blinder of my own design.
jokes.setup: Ron preparing for Tammy encounter
jokes.punchline: Has engineered a device to prevent sexual temptation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron solution - over-engineered device for emotional problem

jokes.text: She survived on rats and rainwater.
jokes.setup: Describing Tammy camping in his attic
jokes.punchline: Extreme survival details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Tammy's predatory determination

jokes.text: Come work for me in Pawnee?
jokes.setup: Tom building up to ask Lucy on a date
jokes.punchline: Offers her a job instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Tom's inability to be emotionally direct

jokes.text: Hey, this is why they call it Beantown, huh?
jokes.setup: Tom deflecting after learning Lucy has a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Wrong nickname for Chicago (that's Boston)
jokes.explanation: Tom's geographical confusion while trying to play off rejection

jokes.text: Literally. All the steak and whiskey, I have to wear a diaper.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining health problems from relationship
jokes.punchline: Has reached infantile state
jokes.explanation: Gross but effective way to show how Tammy has infantilized him

jokes.text: There's a prize inside for you.
jokes.setup: Tammy trying to seduce Jamm
jokes.punchline: Implies something is literally inside her body
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross and uncomfortable Tammy seduction attempt

jokes.text: Turns out the crotch blinder was inside you all along.
jokes.setup: Jamm successfully resisting Tammy
jokes.punchline: Parodies 'the real treasure was friendship' trope
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of inspirational clichés applied to ridiculous situation

jokes.text: Saturn's Return.
jokes.setup: April describing her quarter-life crisis
jokes.punchline: Donna has an astrological explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna moment - knows random cosmic knowledge. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: When I was your age, I got banned from every riverboat in Germany.
jokes.setup: Donna explaining Saturn's Return to April
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna line - mysterious, specific, and completely unexplained backstory

jokes.text: No, I'm not. I've never cared for you.
jokes.setup: Ben says April is his friend
jokes.punchline: April immediately denies caring in typical fashion
jokes.explanation: Classic April denial of affection that we know isn't true

jokes.text: Although, if I had to pick one side, it would be Lucy Liu's backside.
jokes.setup: Jamm saying he can't choose sides
jokes.punchline: Makes crude pun about celebrity
jokes.explanation: Typical Jamm crude humor that fits character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I always just put six. So far, so good. Dude, you are totally going to jail.
jokes.setup: Tom admitting tax form ignorance
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact prediction of imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from tax confusion to prison prediction

jokes.text: Five-second rule!
jokes.setup: Andy finding leftover spaghetti
jokes.punchline: Applies hygiene rule to restaurant food
jokes.explanation: Simple Andy moment showing his food obsession and lack of hygiene standards
